summary: Re-linked several `dolphin/dvd` objects by marking `dvdfs.c`, `dvd.c`, `dvdqueue.c`, and `fstload.c` as `Matching` in `configure.py`, with small source and split adjustments needed to preserve layout. The source tweaks changed unused match-padding/bugfix guards in `dvd.c` and `fstload.c`, and forced `issueCommand` inline in `dvd.c`; split boundaries for DVD `.data`/`.sdata` were tightened accordingly.
what_changed: `configure.py` changed `dolphin/dvd/dvdfs.c`, `dolphin/dvd/dvd.c`, `dolphin/dvd/dvdqueue.c`, and `dolphin/dvd/fstload.c` from `Object(NonMatching, ...)` to `Object(Matching, ...)` inside the `dvd` runtime library object list.
`src/dolphin/dvd/dvd.c` changed an unused `.bss` padding block guard from `#ifdef MUST_MATCH` to `#ifndef BUGFIX`.
`src/dolphin/dvd/dvd.c` changed `issueCommand(s32 prio, DVDCommandBlock* block)` from `static bool` to `static inline bool`.
`src/dolphin/dvd/fstload.c` changed the unused assignment block around `OSGetArenaHi()` from `#ifdef MUST_MATCH` to `#ifndef BUGFIX`.
smart_moves: Promoted DVD objects to `Matching` only after reconciling both source-level codegen details and section split boundaries.
Used `#ifndef BUGFIX` for known unused original-code artifacts, preserving matching behavior by default while allowing bugfix builds to omit them.
Made `issueCommand` explicitly `static inline`, a minimal source annotation likely used to reproduce the expected call/inline codegen without larger control-flow rewrites.
Updated `splits.txt` to reflect exact section ownership changes rather than leaving stale `.data` or `.sdata` ranges after re-linking.
decomp_lessons: When re-linking formerly nonmatching Dolphin SDK objects, check both object matching status and adjacent split boundaries; small data-section size changes can be part of the fix.
Unused variables, padding, or assignments may need to remain in non-bugfix builds for matching even when they look removable from a cleanup perspective.
Prefer narrow compiler-behavior hints such as `static inline` when they solve matching for a specific helper function.
Changing objects from `NonMatching` to `Matching` should be paired with source guards that preserve retail layout while still supporting `BUGFIX` configurations.
naming_conventions: `BUGFIX` is used as a conditional build macro for excluding artifacts that appear necessary for original matching but undesirable in fixed builds.
`MUST_MATCH` was replaced in these cases by `#ifndef BUGFIX`, suggesting a convention of keeping retail-matching quirks unless explicitly building with bug fixes.
The affected DVD helper and callback names follow existing Dolphin style: `cbForStateBusy`, `issueCommand`, `__fstLoad`, `DVDCommandBlock`, `OSGetArenaHi`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Marking `issueCommand` as `static inline` is the main visible codegen tactic in the diff.
Preserving an unused `.bss` allocation in `dvd.c` under `#ifndef BUGFIX` maintains section layout for matching.
Preserving an unused `OSGetArenaHi()` assignment block in `fstload.c` under `#ifndef BUGFIX` maintains expected code or data behavior for matching.
Section split endpoints were adjusted by small byte counts, indicating careful relink validation around `.data` and `.sdata` placement.
follow_up_queries: Check later PRs touching `dolphin/dvd/dvderror.c` or `dvdlow.c` for remaining DVD runtime matching work.
Search for other replacements of `#ifdef MUST_MATCH` with `#ifndef BUGFIX` to understand the project-wide convention.
Review build artifacts around `issueCommand` to confirm whether the `static inline` annotation was required for inlining or section ordering.
Audit adjacent `splits.txt` DVD entries when changing `.data` or `.sdata` boundaries in future re-link work.
